license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.t3geqjw
title: Dissociation of Single Cell Suspensions from Human Breast Tissues
author: Shanshan Bai

steps_raw.instruction: Dissolve 0.04 g, collagenase A in 4 mL, DMEM F12/HEPES media .
4 mL, 10xcollagenase DMEMF12/HEPES 26 mL, DMEMF12/HEPES 10 mL, BSA fraction V
In a 10 cm dish, place a 1-3cm 3 tissue section and add ~ 5 mL, DS . Mince the tissue with scalpels into 1 mm 3 pieces.
Transfer minced tissue to a 50 ml conical tube. Q.S. to 35 ml with DS, enough to cover all the tissue.
Two options: Rapid-dissociation or Exhaustive Dissociation

Gently dissociate the suspension at 37 °C in a hybridization oven for 00:15:00 - 00:30:00 . With rotator set to medium (optional: pipet up and down with a 5 ml pipette to aid digestion during the incubation period).

Place the 70 μm filter on top of a 50 ml conical tube and filter the tissue suspension. Use a syringe plunger flange to grind the leftover unfiltered tissue. Rinse the filter with DMEM to ensure any remaining single cells are filtered. If necessary, re-filter the solution one more time.
Spin at 430 xg for 00:05:00 .

Remove the top adipocyte layer and the rest of the supernatant. The adipocyte layer can be saved for future analysis by storage in 90% DMSO and 10% FBS. However, the adipocyte cells are usually larger than 50 microns and can therefore not be loaded into the 10X genomics microfluidic chips.
Resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL, DMEM .
Pass cell suspension through a 40 μm flowmi filter to a 1.5 ml tube.
Centrifuge cells at 4 °C 300 xg for 00:05:00 . If no RBCs are visible, skip directly onto step 19 .

Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in: 1x MACS RBC lysis buffer. ***RBC lysis can be done up to 2 times.*** You can check the cells under a light microscope to see if RBCs are still present after each lysis step.
Incubate nutating at RT for 00:10:00 in 10 mL - 20 mL, 1X buffer .
Add 2X the volume of DMEM into the tube and centrifuge cells at 4 °C 300 xg for 00:05:00 .
Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL, cold PBS .
Filter with a 40 μl flowmi into a 1.5 ml tube.
Spin at 300 xg for 00:05:00 .
After centrifugation, remove the supernatant and wash cells with 1 mL, cold PBS .
Centrifuge cells at 4 °C 300 xg for 00:05:00 .
Remove the supernatant.
Resuspend cell pellet in PBS+ 0.04% BSA. Adjust cell concentration to 700-1200 cells/μl.
To check for nucleated cells : Make a 1:1 solution of Hoechst and PI, pipette 2 µL of this mix to 10 µL, filtered cells and count in the Countess II FL (ThermoFisher) or a Hemocytometer.
To check for viability : Mix 10 µL, Trypan blue + 10 µL, cells . Load 10 µL, of the cell suspension for counting using a Hemocytomer or the Countess II (ThermoFisher)

Gently dissociate the suspension at 37 °C in a hybridization oven for 12:00:00 . With rotator set to medium (optional: pipet up and down with a 5 ml pipette to aid digestion during the incubation period).

Place the 70 μm filter on top of a 50 ml conical tube and filter the tissue suspension. Use a syringe plunger flange to grind the leftover unfiltered tissue. Rinse the filter with DMEM to ensure any remaining single cells are filtered. If necessary, re-filter the solution one more time.

Add 2X the volume of DMEM into the tube and centrifuge cells at 4 °C 300xg for 00:05:00 .
Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 1 µL, cold PBS .
Filter with a 40 μl flowmi into a 1.5 ml tube.
